title: Multiple-choice cloze
description: Read the text below and decide which answer (A, B, C or D) best fits each gap.

content.text: In recent years, the concept of “digital minimalism” has gained traction among psychologists and sociologists. Early commentators (0) it as a passing trend, but growing evidence suggests it reflects a deeper shift in how people relate to technology.||Digital minimalism does not (1) abandoning devices altogether; rather, it encourages individuals to (2) their use of technology so that it serves clearly defined values. Supporters argue that this approach can (3) feelings of anxiety and distraction that often accompany constant connectivity.||One key principle is to be highly selective about online activities. Instead of (4) mindlessly through social media feeds, adherents choose a limited number of platforms that genuinely (5) their goals. This intentionality can help users (6) the temptation to check their phones whenever they experience a moment of boredom.||Critics, however, warn that digital minimalism may (7) those who lack the resources or flexibility to disconnect, such as gig workers who depend on apps for income. Nevertheless, its advocates maintain that even small adjustments can (8) a noticeable improvement in wellbeing, provided they are sustained over time.

example.explanation: The expression “dismissed it as a passing trend” is a fixed collocation. “Framed” and “enacted” do not fit with ‘trend’ in this sense, and “appointed” is used for positions or people, not ideas.

questions.explanation: The correct expression is “does not entail abandoning devices”. “Entail” is used to indicate that something implies something else. “Revolve” and “portray” do not work with ‘devices’, and “abandon” would change the meaning.
“Curate their use of technology” is a common modern collocation. “Scatter” and “seize” don't fit together semantically, and “resume” would imply going back to something, not managing it carefully.

The collocation is “alleviate feelings of anxiety”. “Heighten” and “trigger” imply increasing them, the opposite of the meaning, and “foster feelings of anxiety” would also be negative, contrary to the message of the text.
The fixed expression is “scrolling mindlessly through social media feeds”. “Drifting” and “roaming” are not used with ‘feeds’ in this context, and “pacing” refers to walking, not screen use.
“Support their goals” is the natural placement. “Comply their goals” is grammatically incorrect, “collide” implies conflict and “offset” is used with costs or effects, not with 'goals'.

The expression is “resist the temptation.” “Surrender the temptation” is not idiomatic, “embrace” would be the opposite (accept the temptation) and “pursue” does not fit with ‘temptation’ in this context either.

The text suggests that it may harm certain groups: “may marginalize those who lack the resources…”. “Overlook” would refer to ignoring, “empower” is positive and “burden” does not fit so well with the idea of ​​social exclusion.
"Produce a noticeable improvement" means to cause or create one. "Require" implies needing it, "reflect" implies showing signs of it, and "sustain" means maintaining it, not creating it.
